title: Scalar upwind update is monotone under the CFL condition

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Ch. 4 and Sec. 6.4.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If 0 <= cfl <= 1, uLeft <= vLeft, and u <= v, then the scalar upwind update at (uLeft, u) is less than or equal to the update at (vLeft, v).
informal.latex: If \(0\le\lambda\le1\), \(u_L\le v_L\), and \(u\le v\), then \((1-\lambda)u+\lambda u_L\le(1-\lambda)v+\lambda v_L\).
assumptions: cfl, uLeft, u, vLeft, and v are real numbers
informal.expected_conclusion: The scalar upwind update is monotone in both input values under the CFL condition.

declarations.name: upwindStep
declarations.statement: def upwindStep (cfl uLeft u : ℝ) : ℝ := (1 - cfl) * u + cfl * uLeft

declarations.name: upwindStep_monotone
declarations.statement: theorem upwindStep_monotone (cfl uLeft u vLeft v : ℝ) (hcfl0 : 0 ≤ cfl) (hcfl1 : cfl ≤ 1) (hLeft : uLeft ≤ vLeft) (h : u ≤ v) : upwindStep cfl uLeft u ≤ upwindStep cfl vLeft v

tasks.input: Translate the scalar upwind monotonicity statement into Lean 4 using upwindStep.
tasks.target: theorem upwindStep_monotone (cfl uLeft u vLeft v : ℝ) (hcfl0 : 0 ≤ cfl) (hcfl1 : cfl ≤ 1) (hLeft : uLeft ≤ vLeft) (h : u ≤ v) : upwindStep cfl uLeft u ≤ upwindStep cfl vLeft v

tasks.input: Given upwindStep and the theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using nonnegative weights, mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left, and linarith.

tasks.input: Find the Mathlib lemmas needed to prove monotonicity of a two-term convex combination.
